per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Well, I've been feeling a little lightheaded during some of the episodes, but nothing too extreme. And my wife says I'm sweating a lot more than usual when it happens, but that's about it, I think.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, like I said, my wife says I'm sweating a lot more than usual when it happens. And yeah, I've felt lightheaded a few times, but it's not too bad... yet. (pauses)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I've noticed that lately, my ankles have been feeling a bit swollen too. Not super painful or anything, but they feel tighter in my shoes than usual. And sometimes I get this tingling sensation in my left arm when the chest pressure gets bad enough. It's weird.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, like I said, my ankles have been feeling a bit swollen lately. And to be honest, it's not just the ankles - sometimes my feet feel puffy too, especially at the end of a long day on the road driving. But again, it's not painful or anything, just feels like they're swelling up a bit more than usual.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yeah... I've had some mild nausea during those episodes, but nothing too severe. It's just a feeling of queasiness in my stomach that goes away once the pressure subsides.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My dad actually had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away. It's been on my mind lately, especially with what I'm going through now. But other than that, my mom is still doing okay at 78, she's got some health issues like hypertension and diabetes, but she's managing them well.

(waiting for the student to ask more questions or proceed with the conversation)
